NKJV.1: Why do You stand afar off, O LORD? Why do You hide in times of trouble?
NKJV.2: The wicked in his pride persecutes the poor; Let them be caught in the plots which they have devised.
NKJV.3: For the wicked boasts of his heart’s desire; He blesses the greedy and renounces the LORD.
NKJV.4: The wicked in his proud countenance does not seek God; God is in none of his thoughts.
NKJV.5: His ways are always prospering; Your judgments are far above, out of his sight; As for all his enemies, he sneers at them.
NKJV.6: He has said in his heart, “I shall not be moved;  I shall never be in adversity.”
NKJV.7: His mouth is full of cursing and deceit and oppression; Under his tongue is trouble and iniquity.
NKJV.8: He sits in the lurking places of the villages; In the secret places he murders the innocent; His eyes are secretly fixed on the helpless.
NKJV.9: He lies in wait secretly, as a lion in his den; He lies in wait to catch the poor; He catches the poor when he draws him into his net.
NKJV.10: So he crouches, he lies low, That the helpless may fall by his strength.
NKJV.11: He has said in his heart, “God has forgotten; He hides His face; He will never see.”
NKJV.12: Arise, O LORD! O God, lift up Your hand! Do not forget the humble.
NKJV.13: Why do the wicked renounce God? He has said in his heart, “You will not require an account.”
NKJV.14: But You have seen, for You observe trouble and grief, To repay it by Your hand. The helpless commits himself to You; You are the helper of the fatherless.
NKJV.15: Break the arm of the wicked and the evil man; Seek out his wickedness until You find none.
NKJV.16: The LORD is King forever and ever; The nations have perished out of His land.
NKJV.17: LORD, You have heard the desire of the humble; You will prepare their heart; You will cause Your ear to hear,
NKJV.18: To do justice to the fatherless and the oppressed, That the man of the earth may oppress no more.

KJV.1: Why standest<S>5975</S> thou afar off<S>7350</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>? why hidest<S>5956</S> thou thyself in times<S>6256</S> of trouble<S>6869</S>?
KJV.2: The wicked<S>7563</S> in his pride<S>1346</S> doth persecute<S>1814</S> the poor<S>6041</S>: let them be taken<S>8610</S> in the devices<S>4209</S> that<S>2098</S> they have imagined<S>2803</S>. <sup>The wicked: Heb. In the pride of the wicked he doth persecute</sup>
KJV.3: For the wicked<S>7563</S> boasteth<S>1984</S> of his heart's<S>5315</S> desire<S>8378</S>, and blesseth<S>1288</S> the covetous<S>1214</S>, whom the LORD<S>3068</S> abhorreth<S>5006</S>. <sup>heart's: Heb. soul's</sup> <sup>blesseth: or, the covetous blesseth himself, he abhorreth the LORD</sup>
KJV.4: The wicked<S>7563</S>, through the pride<S>1363</S> of his countenance<S>639</S>, will not seek<S>1875</S> after God: God<S>430</S> is not in all his thoughts<S>4209</S>. <sup>God is: or, all his thoughts are, There is no God</sup>
KJV.5: His ways<S>1870</S> are always<S>6256</S> grievous<S>2342</S>; thy judgments<S>4941</S> are far above<S>4791</S> out of his sight: as for all his enemies<S>6887</S>, he puffeth<S>6315</S> at them.
KJV.6: He hath said<S>559</S> in his heart<S>3820</S>, I shall not be moved<S>4131</S>: for I shall never<S>1755</S> <S>1755</S> be in adversity<S>7451</S>. <sup>never: Heb. unto generation and generation</sup>
KJV.7: His mouth<S>6310</S> is full<S>4390</S> of cursing<S>423</S> and deceit<S>4820</S> and fraud<S>8496</S>: under his tongue<S>3956</S> is mischief<S>5999</S> and vanity<S>205</S>. <sup>deceit: Heb. deceits</sup> <sup>vanity: or, iniquity</sup>
KJV.8: He sitteth<S>3427</S> in the lurking places<S>3993</S> of the villages<S>2691</S>: in the secret places<S>4565</S> doth he murder<S>2026</S> the innocent<S>5355</S>: his eyes<S>5869</S> are privily set<S>6845</S> against the poor<S>2489</S>. <sup>are: Heb. hide themselves</sup>
KJV.9: He lieth in wait<S>693</S> secretly<S>4565</S> as a lion<S>738</S> in his den<S>5520</S>: he lieth in wait<S>693</S> to catch<S>2414</S> the poor<S>6041</S>: he doth catch<S>2414</S> the poor<S>6041</S>, when he draweth<S>4900</S> him into his net<S>7568</S>. <sup>secretly: Heb. in the secret places</sup>
KJV.10: He croucheth<S>1794</S>, and humbleth<S>7817</S> himself, that the poor<S>2426</S> <S>2489</S> <S>2489</S> may fall<S>5307</S> by his strong ones<S>6099</S>. <sup>He: Heb. He breaketh himself</sup> <sup>by: or, into his strong parts</sup>
KJV.11: He hath said<S>559</S> in his heart<S>3820</S>, God<S>410</S> hath forgotten<S>7911</S>: he hideth<S>5641</S> his face<S>6440</S>; he will never<S>5331</S> see<S>7200</S> it.
KJV.12: Arise<S>6965</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>; O God<S>410</S>, lift up<S>5375</S> thine hand<S>3027</S>: forget<S>7911</S> not the humble<S>6035</S> <S>6041</S>. <sup>humble: or, afflicted</sup>
KJV.13: Wherefore doth the wicked<S>7563</S> contemn<S>5006</S> God<S>430</S>? he hath said<S>559</S> in his heart<S>3820</S>, Thou wilt not require<S>1875</S> it.
KJV.14: Thou hast seen<S>7200</S> it; for thou beholdest<S>5027</S> mischief<S>5999</S> and spite<S>3708</S>, to requite<S>5414</S> it with thy hand<S>3027</S>: the poor<S>2489</S> committeth<S>5800</S> himself unto thee; thou art the helper<S>5826</S> of the fatherless<S>3490</S>. <sup>committeth: Heb. leaveth</sup>
KJV.15: Break<S>7665</S> thou the arm<S>2220</S> of the wicked<S>7563</S> and the evil<S>7451</S> man: seek out<S>1875</S> his wickedness<S>7562</S> till thou find<S>4672</S> none<S>1077</S>.
KJV.16: The LORD<S>3068</S> is King<S>4428</S> for ever<S>5769</S> and ever<S>5703</S>: the heathen<S>1471</S> are perished<S>6</S> out of his land<S>776</S>.
KJV.17: LORD<S>3068</S>, thou hast heard<S>8085</S> the desire<S>8378</S> of the humble<S>6035</S>: thou wilt prepare<S>3559</S> their heart<S>3820</S>, thou wilt cause thine ear<S>241</S> to hear<S>7181</S>: <sup>prepare: or, establish</sup>
KJV.18: To judge<S>8199</S> the fatherless<S>3490</S> and the oppressed<S>1790</S>, that the man<S>582</S> of the earth<S>776</S> may no more<S>3254</S> oppress<S>6206</S>. <sup>oppress: or, terrify</sup>

ESV.1: Why, O LORD, do you stand far away? Why do you hide yourself in times of trouble?
ESV.2: In arrogance the wicked hotly pursue the poor; let them be caught in the schemes that they have devised.
ESV.3: For the wicked boasts of the desires of his soul, and the one greedy for gain curses  and renounces the LORD.
ESV.4: In the pride of his face  the wicked does not seek him;  all his thoughts are, “There is no God.”
ESV.5: His ways prosper at all times; your judgments are on high, out of his sight; as for all his foes, he puffs at them.
ESV.6: He says in his heart, “I shall not be moved; throughout all generations I shall not meet adversity.”
ESV.7: His mouth is filled with cursing and deceit and oppression; under his tongue are mischief and iniquity.
ESV.8: He sits in ambush in the villages; in hiding places he murders the innocent. His eyes stealthily watch for the helpless;
ESV.9: he lurks in ambush like a lion in his thicket; he lurks that he may seize the poor; he seizes the poor when he draws him into his net.
ESV.10: The helpless are crushed, sink down, and fall by his might.
ESV.11: He says in his heart, “God has forgotten, he has hidden his face, he will never see it.”
ESV.12: Arise, O LORD; O God, lift up your hand; forget not the afflicted.
ESV.13: Why does the wicked renounce God and say in his heart, “You will not call to account”?
ESV.14: But you do see, for you note mischief and vexation, that you may take it into your hands; to you the helpless commits himself; you have been the helper of the fatherless.
ESV.15: Break the arm of the wicked and evildoer; call his wickedness to account till you find none.
ESV.16: The LORD is king forever and ever; the nations perish from his land.
ESV.17: O LORD, you hear the desire of the afflicted; you will strengthen their heart; you will incline your ear
ESV.18: to do justice to the fatherless and the oppressed, so that man who is of the earth may strike terror no more.

NASB.1: Why do You stand afar off, O LORD? Why do You hide [Yourself] in times of trouble?
NASB.2: In pride the wicked hotly pursue the afflicted; Let them be caught in the plots which they have devised.
NASB.3: For the wicked boasts of his heart's desire, And the greedy man curses [and] spurns the LORD.
NASB.4: The wicked, in the haughtiness of his countenance, does not seek [Him]. All his thoughts are, 'There is no God.'
NASB.5: His ways prosper at all times; Your judgments are on high, out of his sight; As for all his adversaries, he snorts at them.
NASB.6: He says to himself, 'I will not be moved; Throughout all generations I will not be in adversity.'
NASB.7: His mouth is full of curses and deceit and oppression; Under his tongue is mischief and wickedness.
NASB.8: He sits in the lurking places of the villages; In the hiding places he kills the innocent; His eyes stealthily watch for the unfortunate.
NASB.9: He lurks in a hiding place as a lion in his lair; He lurks to catch the afflicted; He catches the afflicted when he draws him into his net.
NASB.10: He crouches, he bows down, And the unfortunate fall by his mighty ones.
NASB.11: He says to himself, 'God has forgotten; He has hidden His face; He will never see it.'
NASB.12: Arise, O LORD; O God, lift up Your hand. Do not forget the afflicted.
NASB.13: Why has the wicked spurned God? He has said to himself, 'You will not require [it].'
NASB.14: You have seen [it], for You have beheld mischief and vexation to take it into Your hand. The unfortunate commits [himself] to You; You have been the helper of the orphan.
NASB.15: Break the arm of the wicked and the evildoer, Seek out his wickedness until You find none.
NASB.16: The LORD is King forever and ever; Nations have perished from His land.
NASB.17: O LORD, You have heard the desire of the humble; You will strengthen their heart, You will incline Your ear
NASB.18: To vindicate the orphan and the oppressed, So that man who is of the earth will no longer cause terror.

NLT.1:
O LORD, why do you stand so far away?
Why do you hide when I am in trouble?
NLT.2:
The wicked arrogantly hunt down the poor.
Let them be caught in the evil they plan for others.
NLT.3:
For they brag about their evil desires;
they praise the greedy and curse the LORD.
NLT.4:
The wicked are too proud to seek God.
They seem to think that God is dead.
NLT.5:
Yet they succeed in everything they do.
They do not see your punishment awaiting them.
They sneer at all their enemies.
NLT.6:
They think, “Nothing bad will ever happen to us!
We will be free of trouble forever!”
NLT.7:
Their mouths are full of cursing, lies, and threats.
Trouble and evil are on the tips of their tongues.
NLT.8:
They lurk in ambush in the villages,
waiting to murder innocent people.
They are always searching for helpless victims.
NLT.9:
Like lions crouched in hiding,
they wait to pounce on the helpless.
Like hunters they capture the helpless
and drag them away in nets.
NLT.10:
Their helpless victims are crushed;
they fall beneath the strength of the wicked.
NLT.11:
The wicked think, “God isn’t watching us!
He has closed his eyes and won’t even see what we do!”
NLT.12:
Arise, O LORD!
Punish the wicked, O God!
Do not ignore the helpless!
NLT.13:
Why do the wicked get away with despising God?
They think, “God will never call us to account.”
NLT.14:
But you see the trouble and grief they cause.
You take note of it and punish them.
The helpless put their trust in you.
You defend the orphans.
NLT.15:
Break the arms of these wicked, evil people!
Go after them until the last one is destroyed.
NLT.16:
The LORD is king forever and ever!
The godless nations will vanish from the land.
NLT.17:
LORD, you know the hopes of the helpless.
Surely you will hear their cries and comfort them.
NLT.18:
You will bring justice to the orphans and the oppressed,
so mere people can no longer terrify them.

WEB.1: Why do you stand far off, Yahweh? Why do you hide yourself in times of trouble?
WEB.2: In arrogance, the wicked hunt down the weak. They are caught in the schemes that they devise.
WEB.3: For the wicked boasts of his heart’s cravings. He blesses the greedy, and condemns Yahweh.
WEB.4: The wicked, in the pride of his face, has no room in his thoughts for God.
WEB.5: His ways are prosperous at all times. He is haughty, and your laws are far from his sight. As for all his adversaries, he sneers at them.
WEB.6: He says in his heart, “I shall not be shaken. For generations I shall have no trouble.”
WEB.7: His mouth is full of cursing, deceit, and oppression. Under his tongue is mischief and iniquity.
WEB.8: He lies in wait near the villages. From ambushes, he murders the innocent. His eyes are secretly set against the helpless.
WEB.9: He lurks in secret as a lion in his ambush. He lies in wait to catch the helpless. He catches the helpless, when he draws him in his net.
WEB.10: The helpless are crushed. They collapse. They fall under his strength.
WEB.11: He says in his heart, “God has forgotten. He hides his face. He will never see it.”
WEB.12: Arise, Yahweh! God, lift up your hand! Don’t forget the helpless.
WEB.13: Why does the wicked person condemn God, and say in his heart, “God won’t call me into account?”
WEB.14: But you do see trouble and grief. You consider it to take it into your hand. You help the victim and the fatherless.
WEB.15: Break the arm of the wicked. As for the evil man, seek out his wickedness until you find none.
WEB.16: Yahweh is King forever and ever! The nations will perish out of his land.
WEB.17: Yahweh, you have heard the desire of the humble. You will prepare their heart. You will cause your ear to hear,
WEB.18: to judge the fatherless and the oppressed, that man who is of the earth may terrify no more.

CSB17.1:
LORD, [1] [2] why do you stand so far away? ⓕ
Why do you hide in times of trouble? ⓖ
CSB17.2:
In arrogance the wicked relentlessly pursue their victims;
let them be caught in the schemes they have devised. ⓗ
CSB17.3:
For the wicked one boasts about his own cravings; ⓘ
the one who is greedy curses [3] and despises the LORD. ⓙ
CSB17.4:
In all his scheming,
the wicked person arrogantly thinks, [4]
“There’s no accountability,
since there’s no God.” ⓚ
CSB17.5:
His ways are always secure; [5]
your lofty judgments have no effect on him; [6]
he scoffs at all his adversaries. ⓛ
CSB17.6:
He says to himself, “I will never be moved —
from generation to generation without calamity.” ⓜ
CSB17.7:
Cursing, deceit, and violence fill his mouth;
trouble and malice are under his tongue. ⓝ
CSB17.8:
He waits in ambush near settlements; ⓐ
he kills the innocent in secret places.
His eyes are on the lookout for the helpless; ⓑ
CSB17.9:
he lurks in secret like a lion in a thicket.
He lurks in order to seize a victim;
he seizes a victim and drags him in his net.
CSB17.10:
So he is oppressed and beaten down;
helpless people fall because of the wicked one’s strength. ⓒ
CSB17.11:
He says to himself, “God has forgotten;
he hides his face and will never see.” ⓓ
CSB17.12:
Rise up, LORD God! Lift up your hand. ⓔ
Do not forget the oppressed. ⓕ
CSB17.13:
Why has the wicked person despised God?
He says to himself, “You will not demand an account.” ⓖ
CSB17.14:
But you yourself have seen trouble and grief,
observing it in order to take the matter into your hands. ⓗ
The helpless one entrusts himself to you;
you are a helper of the fatherless. ⓘ
CSB17.15:
Break the arm of the wicked, evil person, ⓙ
until you look for his wickedness,
but it can’t be found. ⓚ
CSB17.16:
The LORD is King forever and ever; ⓛ
the nations will perish from his land. ⓜ
CSB17.17:
LORD, you have heard the desire of the humble;
you will strengthen their hearts.
You will listen carefully, ⓝ
CSB17.18:
doing justice for the fatherless and the oppressed
so that mere humans from the earth may terrify them no more. ⓐ

AMP.1:
Why do You stand far away, O LORD?
Why do You hide [Yourself, veiling Your eyes] in times of trouble?
AMP.2:
In pride and arrogance the wicked hotly pursue and persecute the afflicted;
Let them be caught in the plots which they have devised.
AMP.3:
For the wicked boasts and sings the praises of his heart's desire,
And the greedy man curses and spurns [and even despises] the LORD.
AMP.4:
The wicked, in the haughtiness of his face, will not seek nor inquire for Him;
All his thoughts are, "There is no God [so there is no accountability or punishment]."
AMP.5:
His ways prosper at all times;
Your judgments [LORD] are on high, out of his sight [so he never thinks about them];
As for all his enemies, he sneers at them.
AMP.6:
He says to himself, "I will not be moved;
For throughout all generations I will not be in adversity [for nothing bad will happen to me]."
AMP.7:
His mouth is full of curses and deceit (fraud) and oppression;
Under his tongue is mischief and wickedness [injustice and sin].
AMP.8:
He lurks in ambush in the villages;
In hiding places he kills the innocent;
He lies in wait for the unfortunate [the unhappy, the poor, the helpless].
AMP.9:
He lurks in a hiding place like a lion in his lair;
He lies in wait to catch the afflicted;
He catches the afflicted when he draws him into his net.
AMP.10:
He crushes [his prey] and crouches;
And the unfortunate fall by his mighty claws.
AMP.11:
He says to himself, "God has [quite] forgotten;
He has hidden His face; He will never see my deed."
AMP.12:
Arise, O LORD! O God, lift up Your hand [in judgment];
Do not forget the suffering.
AMP.13:
Why has the wicked spurned and shown disrespect to God?
He has said to himself, "You will not require me to account."
AMP.14:
You have seen it, for You have noted mischief and vexation (irritation) to take it into Your hand.
The unfortunate commits himself to You;
You are the helper of the fatherless.
AMP.15:
Break the arm of the wicked and the evildoer,
Seek out his wickedness until You find no more.
AMP.16:
The LORD is King forever and ever;
The nations will perish from His land.
AMP.17:
O LORD, You have heard the desire of the humble and oppressed;
You will strengthen their heart, You will incline Your ear to hear,
AMP.18:
To vindicate and obtain justice for the fatherless and the oppressed,
So that man who is of the earth will no longer terrify them.

MSG.1:
God, are you avoiding me?
Where are you when I need you?
MSG.2:
Full of hot air, the wicked
are hot on the trail of the poor.
Trip them up, tangle them up
in their fine-tuned plots.
MSG.3:
The wicked are windbags,
the swindlers have foul breath.
MSG.4:
The wicked snub God,
their noses stuck high in the air.
Their graffiti are scrawled on the walls:
"Catch us if you can!" "God is dead."
MSG.5:
They care nothing for what you think;
if you get in their way, they blow you off.
MSG.6:
They live (they think) a charmed life:
"We can't go wrong. This is our lucky year!"
MSG.7:
They carry a mouthful of hexes,
their tongues spit venom like adders.
MSG.8:
They hide behind ordinary people,
then pounce on their victims.
MSG.9:
They mark the luckless,
then wait like a hunter in a blind;
When the poor wretch wanders too close,
they stab him in the back.
MSG.10:
The hapless fool is kicked to the ground,
the unlucky victim is brutally axed.
MSG.11:
He thinks God has dumped him,
he's sure that God is indifferent to his plight.
MSG.12:
Time to get up, God—get moving.
The luckless think they're Godforsaken.
MSG.13:
They wonder why the wicked scorn God
and get away with it,
Why the wicked are so cocksure
they'll never come up for audit.
MSG.14:
But you know all about it—
the contempt, the abuse.
I dare to believe that the luckless
will get lucky someday in you.
You won't let them down:
orphans won't be orphans forever.
MSG.15:
Break the wicked right arms,
break all the evil left arms.
Search and destroy
every sign of crime.
MSG.16:
God's grace and order wins;
godlessness loses.
MSG.17:
The victim's faint pulse picks up;
the hearts of the hopeless pump red blood
as you put your ear to their lips.
MSG.18:
Orphans get parents,
the homeless get homes.
The reign of terror is over,
the rule of the gang lords is ended.

NIV.1:
Psalm 10
Why, O Lord, do you stand far off?
Why do you hide yourself in times of trouble?
NIV.2:
In his arrogance the wicked man hunts down the weak,
who are caught in the schemes he devises.
NIV.3:
He boasts of the cravings of his heart;
he blesses the greedy and reviles the Lord.
NIV.4:
In his pride the wicked does not seek him;
in all his thoughts there is no room for God.
NIV.5:
His ways are always prosperous;
he is haughty and your laws are far from him;
he sneers at all his enemies.
NIV.6:
He says to himself, “Nothing will shake me;
I’ll always be happy and never have trouble.”
NIV.7:
His mouth is full of curses and lies and threats;
trouble and evil are under his tongue.
NIV.8:
He lies in wait near the villages;
from ambush he murders the innocent,
watching in secret for his victims.
NIV.9:
He lies in wait like a lion in cover;
he lies in wait to catch the helpless;
he catches the helpless and drags them off in his net.
NIV.10:
His victims are crushed, they collapse;
they fall under his strength.
NIV.11:
He says to himself, “God has forgotten;
he covers his face and never sees.”
NIV.12:
Arise, Lord! Lift up your hand, O God.
Do not forget the helpless.
NIV.13:
Why does the wicked man revile God?
Why does he say to himself,
“He won’t call me to account”?
NIV.14:
But you, O God, do see trouble and grief;
you consider it to take it in hand.
The victim commits himself to you;
you are the helper of the fatherless.
NIV.15:
Break the arm of the wicked and evil man;
call him to account for his wickedness
that would not be found out.
NIV.16:
The Lord is King for ever and ever;
the nations will perish from his land.
NIV.17:
You hear, O Lord, the desire of the afflicted;
you encourage them, and you listen to their cry,
NIV.18:
defending the fatherless and the oppressed,
in order that man, who is of the earth, may terrify no more.

NRSVCE.1:
Why, O LORD, do you stand far off?
Why do you hide yourself in times of trouble?
NRSVCE.2:
In arrogance the wicked persecute the poor —
let them be caught in the schemes they have devised.
NRSVCE.3:
For the wicked boast of the desires of their heart,
those greedy for gain curse and renounce the LORD.
NRSVCE.4:
In the pride of their countenance the wicked say, “God will not seek it out”;
all their thoughts are, “There is no God.”
NRSVCE.5:
Their ways prosper at all times;
your judgments are on high, out of their sight;
as for their foes, they scoff at them.
NRSVCE.6:
They think in their heart, “We shall not be moved;
throughout all generations we shall not meet adversity.”
NRSVCE.7:
Their mouths are filled with cursing and deceit and oppression;
under their tongues are mischief and iniquity.
NRSVCE.8:
They sit in ambush in the villages;
in hiding places they murder the innocent.
Their eyes stealthily watch for the helpless;
NRSVCE.9:
they lurk in secret like a lion in its covert;
they lurk that they may seize the poor;
they seize the poor and drag them off in their net.
NRSVCE.10:
They stoop, they crouch,
and the helpless fall by their might.
NRSVCE.11:
They think in their heart, “God has forgotten,
he has hidden his face, he will never see it.”
NRSVCE.12:
Rise up, O LORD; O God, lift up your hand;
do not forget the oppressed.
NRSVCE.13:
Why do the wicked renounce God,
and say in their hearts, “You will not call us to account”?
NRSVCE.14:
But you do see! Indeed you note trouble and grief,
that you may take it into your hands;
the helpless commit themselves to you;
you have been the helper of the orphan.
NRSVCE.15:
Break the arm of the wicked and evildoers;
seek out their wickedness until you find none.
NRSVCE.16:
The LORD is king forever and ever;
the nations shall perish from his land.
NRSVCE.17:
O LORD, you will hear the desire of the meek;
you will strengthen their heart, you will incline your ear
NRSVCE.18:
to do justice for the orphan and the oppressed,
so that those from earth may strike terror no more.

NET.1: Why, LORD, do you stand far off? Why do you pay no attention during times of trouble?
NET.2: The wicked arrogantly chase the oppressed; the oppressed are trapped by the schemes the wicked have dreamed up.
NET.3: Yes, the wicked man boasts because he gets what he wants; the one who robs others curses and rejects the LORD.
NET.4: The wicked man is so arrogant he always thinks, “God won’t hold me accountable; he doesn’t care.”
NET.5: He is secure at all times. He has no regard for your commands; he disdains all his enemies.
NET.6: He says to himself, “I will never be upended, because I experience no calamity.”
NET.7: His mouth is full of curses and deceptive, harmful words; his tongue injures and destroys.
NET.8: He waits in ambush near the villages; in hidden places he kills the innocent. His eyes look for some unfortunate victim.
NET.9: He lies in ambush in a hidden place, like a lion in a thicket; he lies in ambush, waiting to catch the oppressed; he catches the oppressed by pulling in his net.
NET.10: His victims are crushed and beaten down; they are trapped in his sturdy nets.
NET.11: He says to himself, “God overlooks it; he does not pay attention; he never notices.”
NET.12: Rise up, LORD! O God, strike him down! Do not forget the oppressed!
NET.13: Why does the wicked man reject God? He says to himself, “You will not hold me accountable.”
NET.14: You have taken notice, for you always see one who inflicts pain and suffering. The unfortunate victim entrusts his cause to you; you deliver the fatherless.
NET.15: Break the arm of the wicked and evil man! Hold him accountable for his wicked deeds, which he thought you would not discover.
NET.16: The LORD rules forever! The nations are driven out of his land.
NET.17: LORD, you have heard the request of the oppressed; you make them feel secure because you listen to their prayer.
NET.18: You defend the fatherless and oppressed, so that mere mortals may no longer terrorize them.
